subject: Chemistry
q:
The strength of 50 volume solution of hydrogen peroxide is _____ g/L (Nearest integer).

Given:
Molar mass of H2O2 is 34 g mol-1
Molar volume of gas at STP=22.7 L.
question:
The strength of 50 volume solution of hydrogen peroxide is _____ g/L (Nearest integer).

Given:
Molar mass of H2O2 is 34 g mol-1
Molar volume of gas at STP=22.7 L.
solution:
Molarity =Volume strength of H2O2Molar volume of oxygen gas at STP×2

Molarity =5011.35

The strength of the solution=Molarity×Molecular weight of H2O2

∴ Strength in gm/L=5011.35×34.

subject: Chemistry
q:
The mass of iron converted into $Fe_3O_4$ by the action of $18$ g of steam is :
(Given : Molar mass of H, O and Fe are $1$, $16$ and $56$ g mol$^{-1}$ respectively)
Assume iron is present in excess :
question:
The mass of iron converted into $Fe_3O_4$ by the action of $18$ g of steam is :
(Given : Molar mass of H, O and Fe are $1$, $16$ and $56$ g mol$^{-1}$ respectively)
Assume iron is present in excess :
solution:
The balanced chemical equation for the reaction of iron with steam is:

$3Fe + 4H_2O \rightarrow Fe_3O_4 + 4H_2$

Molar mass of $H_2O = 2 \times 1 + 16 = 18 \text{ g mol}^{-1}$

Number of moles of steam ($H_2O$) given = $\dfrac{18}{18} = 1 \text{ mol}$

From the balanced equation, $4 \text{ moles}$ of $H_2O$ react with $3 \text{ moles}$ of $Fe$.

Therefore, $1 \text{ mole}$ of $H_2O$ will react with $\dfrac{3}{4} \text{ moles}$ of $Fe$.

Mass of iron reacted = Moles of $Fe \times$ Molar mass of $Fe$

Mass of iron = $\dfrac{3}{4} \times 56 = 3 \times 14 = 42 \text{ g}$

Answer: $42$ g

q: The total number of isotopes of hydrogen and number of radioactive isotopes among them, respectively, are
question: The total number of isotopes of hydrogen and number of radioactive isotopes among them, respectively, are
solution:
Hydrogen is the first element in the periodic table and has the atomic number one. Those elements which have the same atomic number but a different mass number are called isotopes. There are three isotopes of hydrogen namely, protium H11, deuterium H12 or D and lastly tritium H13 or T. The isotopes are different because of the different number of neutrons present in them.

Isotopes of Hydrogen:



Among all three isotopes only Tritium is radioactive.

Mass of H2O2 = 2 × 2522.4 × 34 = 75 gram

Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct
Both the statements I and II are correct
Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect
solution: The difference between the zeolite and ion exchange process is that the zeolite process uses the mineral zeolite as the exchanging resin for cations in the hard water whereas the ion exchange process include several different resins for the ion exchange. Synthetic resins method is more efficient as it can exchange both cations and anions.

subject: Chemistry
q:
In basic medium, H2O2 exhibits which of the following reactions?
(A) Mn2+→Mn4+
(B) I2→I-
(C) PbS→PbSO4
Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
question:
In basic medium, H2O2 exhibits which of the following reactions?
(A) Mn2+→Mn4+
(B) I2→I-
(C) PbS→PbSO4
Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
solution:
In basic medium, oxidising action of H2O2.

Mn2++H2O2→Mn+4+2OH-

In basic medium, reducing action of H2O2

I2+H2O2+2OH-→2I-+2H2O+O2

In acidic medium, oxidising action of H2O2

PbS(s)+4H2O2(aq)→PbSO4( s)+4H2O(l)

Hence correct option (4).

q: The reaction of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> with potassium permanganate in acidic medium leads to the formation of mainly
question: The reaction of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> with potassium permanganate in acidic medium leads to the formation of mainly
solution:
When hydrogen peroxide is treated with acidified potassium permanganate, the reduction of permanganate ion to Mn2+ion  takes place by the following reaction,

2KMnO4+3H2SO4+5H2O2→K2SO4+2MnSO4+8H2O+5O2

In the above reaction, potassium permanganate acts an oxidising agent.

q: Which of the following forms of hydrogen emits low energy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">β</mi><mo>-</mo></msup></math> particles?
question: Which of the following forms of hydrogen emits low energy <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">β</mi><mo>-</mo></msup></math> particles?
solution:
Tritium isotope of hydrogen is radioactive and emits low energy β- particles. It is because of high n/p ratio of tritium which makes nucleus unstable.

For tritium H13

No. of neutron n=2

No. of proton p=1

np=2

∵ np is high

tritium wil emit β particle.

subject: Chemistry
q: High purity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mo>></mo><mn>99</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>95</mn><mo>%</mo></mrow></mfenced></math> dihydrogen is obtained by
question: High purity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mo>></mo><mn>99</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>95</mn><mo>%</mo></mrow></mfenced></math> dihydrogen is obtained by
options: reaction of zinc with aqueous alkali.
electrolysis of warm aqueous barium hydroxide solution between nickel electrodes.
electrolysis of acidified water using platinum electrodes.
reaction of zinc with dilute acid.
solution:
Dihydrogen of high purity (>99.95%) is obtained by electrolysing warm aqueous barium hydroxide solution between nickel electrodes. In electrolysis, the reaction occurs at the anode and cathode :-

At anode :- 2OH-→H2O + 12O2 + 2e-

At cathode :- 2H2O + 2e- →2OH- +H2

Hence, the high purity dihydrogen is obtained by electrolysing warm aqueous barium hydroxide.

Around 55% of hydrogen around the world would goes to ammonia production.

question: Hydrogen peroxide oxidises <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi>Fe</mi><msub><mfenced separators="|"><mi>CN</mi></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup><mi>to</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msup><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi>Fe</mi><msub><mfenced separators="|"><mi>CN</mi></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math> in acidic medium, but reduces <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi>Fe</mi><msub><mfenced separators="|"><mi>CN</mi></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup><mi>to</mi><msup><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi>Fe</mi><msub><mfenced separators="|"><mi>CN</mi></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math> in alkaline medium. The other products formed are, respectively
solution:
Hydrogen undergoes the following changes:

1) While acting as an oxidising agent in an acidic medium,

H2O2+2H++2e-→2H2O

2) While acting as a reducing agent in an alkaline medium,

H2O2+2OH-→O2+2H2O+2e-

subject: Chemistry
q: In which of the following reactions the hydrogen peroxide acts as a reducing agent?
question: In which of the following reactions the hydrogen peroxide acts as a reducing agent?
solution:
PbS+4H2O2→PbSO4+4H2O

In the above reaction lead sulphide oxidised to lead sulphate. Hence, hydrogen peroxide acts as oxidising agent.

2Fe2++H2O2→2Fe3++2OH-

In the above reaction Fe2+ oxidised to Fe3+. Hence, hydrogen peroxide acts as oxidising agent.

Mn2++H2O2→Mn4++2OH-

In the above reaction Mn2+ oxidised to Mn4+. Hence, hydrogen peroxide acts as oxidising agent.

HOCl+H2O2→H3O++Cl-1+O2

In the above reaction Cl+1 reduced to Cl-, hence, hydrogen peroxide acts as a reducing agent.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List I with List II

  List I
Type of Hydride		   List II
Example
A Electron deficient hydride I MgH2
B Electron rich hydride II HF
C Electron precise hydride III B2H6
D Saline hydride IV CH4

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
Match List I with List II

  List I
Type of Hydride		   List II
Example
A Electron deficient hydride I MgH2
B Electron rich hydride II HF
C Electron precise hydride III B2H6
D Saline hydride IV CH4

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
solution:
B2H6, known as diborane, is an example of an electron-deficient hydride. HF, or hydrogen fluoride, is an example of an electron-rich hydride. Electron-rich hydrides are molecules that contain more electrons than what would be expected based on the normal valence electron count for the atoms involved. 

CH4 is electrons precise hydride since, there are exact number of electrons to form normal covalent bonds for completing octet.

MgH2 is salline hydride, as this is the hydride that formed when hydrogen reacts with s-Block elements. 

 

A Electron deficient hydride III B2H6
B Electron rich hydride II HF
C Electron precise hydride IV CH4
D Saline hydride I MgH2

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I : Hydrogen peroxide can act as an oxidizing agent in both acidic and basic conditions.

Statement II: Density of hydrogen peroxide at 298 K is lower than that of D2O.

In the light of the above statements. Choose the correct answer from the options.
question:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I : Hydrogen peroxide can act as an oxidizing agent in both acidic and basic conditions.

Statement II: Density of hydrogen peroxide at 298 K is lower than that of D2O.

In the light of the above statements. Choose the correct answer from the options.
options: Both statement I and Statement II are ture
Both statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
It acts as an oxidising as well as reducing agent in both acidic and alkaline media. Simple reactions are described below.

(i) Oxidising action in acidic medium

2Fe2+aq+2H+aq+H2O2aq→2Fe3+aq+2H2Ol

(ii) Reducing action in acidic medium

2MnO4−+6H++5H2O2→2Mn2++8H2O+5O2

(iii) Oxidising action in basic medium

2Fe2++H2O2→2Fe3++2OH-

(iv) Reducing action in basic medium

I2+H2O2+2OH−→2I−+2H2O+O2

The density of hydrogen peroxide is more than that of heavy water

Density of D2O=1.1 g/cc

Density of H2O2=1.45 g/cc
